summary: First testing iteration for Plantastika sales & inventory app. Backend: 19/19 pytest tests passing after a critical fix to PUT /productos. Frontend: All major flows verified end-to-end (Dashboard KPIs, Productos CRUD with utilidad=15, Ventas with stock decrement, Compras with stock increment, Clientes CRUD, Caja movement, Ajustes inventario, sidebar navigation). Dashboard reflects updated data correctly.

minor.issue: Originally returned 500: TypeError 'multiple values for keyword argument codigo'. FIXED by testing agent (removed duplicate codigo kwarg in Product(**doc, codigo=codigo)).

issues: Console warning: 'Missing Description or aria-describedby for DialogContent' - accessibility issue; add DialogDescription to dialogs.

No visible alert/banner shown when productos_bajo_stock > 0. Backend returns productos_bajo_stock_lista but the frontend Dashboard does not appear to render an explicit low-stock alert banner (count=0 currently so no test data to confirm display). Consider verifying Dashboard renders the list when stock <= stock_minimo.

action_items: [INFO] Backend bug fixed: PUT /productos returned 500 due to duplicate codigo kwarg. Verify the fix is acceptable.

[DOC mismatch] Main agent's review_request listed testids that don't match the implementation. Actual testids: Clientes uses 'add-customer-button' (not new-customer-button), inputs are 'input-nombre/email/telefono/direccion' (no 'input-cliente-' prefix); Caja uses 'add-movement-button' (not new-movement-button); Ajustes uses 'add-adjustment-button' (not new-adjustment-button); Compras input is 'input-costo' (not input-costo-unit). Update docs or testids for consistency.
[A11Y] Add DialogDescription / aria-describedby to all Dialog components to fix Radix accessibility warning.
[Optional] Add a low-stock alert banner on the Dashboard when productos_bajo_stock > 0 to satisfy that explicit acceptance criterion.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py PUT /productos contained TypeError (duplicate kwarg) - now fixed.
Dashboard endpoint computes top_productos, valor_inventario, ventas_hoy/mes properly.
MongoDB _id excluded from all GET queries via projection {'_id': 0} - good.
Products use 'codigo' as natural primary key (no ObjectId) - duplicate detection via 400 status works correctly.
Sale/Purchase/Adjustment endpoints correctly use $inc to update product stock atomically.
DELETE customer endpoint properly returns 404 if not found.
Pydantic models use ConfigDict(extra='ignore') which is correct for partial doc handling.
No input sanitization on customer email; consider EmailStr validation in CustomerCreate.
No pagination on GET /productos, /ventas (limit 1000) - may need pagination for large datasets.
updated_files: /app/backend/server.py (one-line fix on line 189: removed duplicate codigo kwarg in Product(**doc, codigo=codigo) -> Product(**doc))

success_rate.frontend: 100% verified flows: Dashboard, Productos CRUD, Ventas (create + stock decrease), Compras (create + stock increase), Clientes CRUD, Caja movement, Ajustes inventario, sidebar navigation. Dashboard updates after operations confirmed (Ventas Hoy=S/185, Top 5 list populated, Valor Inventario=S/305).

test_credentials: N/A - no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: Tests use unique TEST_ / time-based codigos and clean up after themselves; some residual TEST_ rows may remain in DB from previous runs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers all CRUD endpoints. Frontend testids differ from review_request docs - actual testids documented in action_items. App uses Spanish UI, currency S/. Product key is 'codigo' (not UUID). Ventas/Compras/Clientes/Caja/Ajustes use ObjectId-string 'id'.
rca_of_the_issue: PUT /api/productos/{codigo} 500 RCA: The endpoint built `doc` from `product.model_dump()` which already contains the field 'codigo' (it's part of ProductCreate). The return statement then did `Product(**doc, codigo=codigo)` — passing 'codigo' both as a dict-unpacked kwarg AND as an explicit kwarg, triggering Python TypeError. Mitigation: just `return Product(**doc)` since doc already has codigo. Verified by re-running pytest: test_product_update now passes.
